description: Found while fixing fz-rh2.20. annotate_back_edges (jobs/native.rs) builds its SCC graph from Term::TailCall edges ONLY, so a recursion cycle threaded through Term::Call continuations — exactly the shape of count(n-1, acc+1), whose cycle is count__e3 -TailCall-> count__clause_1 -Call/cont-> resume_2 -> resume_3 -TailCall-> count__e3 — lands in separate SCCs and its closing TailCall keeps is_back_edge=false. The cooperative-yield check in emit_native_tail_call (terminator.rs, 'every native loop spends reductions') therefore never fires for such loops. Before fz-rh2.20 this was masked: the synchronous-call bug overflowed the stack before fairness could matter. Now that cont chains are frame-flat (return_call_indirect), a scalar native loop can spin without spending reductions — a scheduler-starvation hazard once concurrent processes share a scheduler. Fix shape: include Term::Call/Term::CallClosure successor edges (callee AND continuation fn) in the SCC graph; widening the edge set only grows SCCs, which only ADDS yield checks — safe direction. Verify with a telemetry-observing test that a count-style loop spends reductions per back edge.
title: Compiler2 native codegen: cont-threaded loops evade back-edge yield checks
